test.command: bun test --max-concurrency=6 apps/dashboard/src apps/api/src packages/api/src packages/auth/src packages/db/src packages/env/src

typecheck.command: bun run check-types && bun x tsc -p apps/dashboard/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/api/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/auth/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/db/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/env/tsconfig.json --noEmit

lint.command: bun x ultracite check apps/dashboard/src apps/api/src packages/api/src packages/auth/src packages/db/src packages/env/src

blockingIssues.description: `apps/dashboard/src/app/onboarding/page.tsx:20` still sends authenticated pending-invite users through onboarding instead of redirecting them to the `/teams` invite-recovery surface, so direct `/onboarding` entry can bypass the required recovery path.

Commit `28a676b` depended on manual edits to ignored `apps/dashboard/.next/types/*` route-type files to make the new `/teams` typed-route literals compile, so the feature is not self-contained from tracked source on a clean checkout.

appliedUpdates.description: Added a validation note documenting that dashboard route changes must regenerate ignored Next typed-route artifacts via `bun x next typegen` / `next dev` / `next build` instead of hand-editing `.next/types/*`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify or enforce that workers cannot mark `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` when required skill invocations or mandatory browser verification steps are missing from the transcript.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviews for `invite-management-and-activation-primitives`, `browser-invite-recovery-surface`, and `team-switcher-and-shell-alignment` all found required skill steps missing (`midday-reference`, `ultracite`, or `agent-browser`) while the worker handoffs still claimed full procedure compliance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align the shared validator contract so worker skills either point to named scoped commands in `.factory/services.yaml` or explicitly allow feature-defined narrower validator scopes when the manifest only contains milestone-wide suites.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both the `invite-management-and-activation-primitives` and `team-switcher-and-shell-alignment` reviews noted that the relevant worker skills say to run scoped validators from `.factory/services.yaml`, but the manifest only defines broader milestone-wide test/lint/typecheck commands, so workers keep falling back to ad hoc feature-specific validator commands.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature lands via an initial commit plus a follow-up fix, record the full feature commit span (or both commit ids) instead of only the last fix commit in the handoff `commitId`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `team-switcher-and-shell-alignment` review found that the handoff recorded only follow-up fix commit `8dfbfe0`, while the primary implementation lived in `0a68b84`, which would be missed by review processes that inspect only `handoff.commitId`.
